channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US and Israel initiate military action against Iran, Trump calls for regime change
item.description: The US and Israel have launched significant military strikes against Iran, marking the start of what President Donald Trump has described as a 'major combat operation.' The strikes, targeting multiple...

item.title: Olivia Dean Wins Big at the 2026 Brit Awards, Taking Home Four Major Trophies
item.description: Olivia Dean had a standout night at the 2026 Brit Awards, taking home four prestigious awards, including Artist of the Year, Album of the Year, Best Pop Act, and Song of the Year. Dean, a graduate of ...

item.title: Josh Simons Resigns Amid Allegations Involving Journalist Investigation
item.description: Josh Simons, the Labour MP for Makerfield, has resigned as a Cabinet Office minister after facing allegations that a think tank he once led commissioned a controversial report into journalists. The re...

item.title: Josh Simons Resigns Amid Controversy Over Investigation into Journalists
item.description: Josh Simons has resigned from his position as Cabinet Office minister after a controversy surrounding a thinktank he previously led. Labour Together, a thinktank Mr Simons ran before entering governme...

item.title: England introduces same-day appointments for urgent GP cases
item.description: The UK government has announced a new contractual requirement for General Practitioners (GPs) in England to provide same-day appointments for patients with urgent health needs. From April, GP practice...

item.title: UK Requires British Passport or Certificate of Entitlement for Dual Nationals Under New ETA Rules
item.description: British dual nationals who do not hold a valid British passport may be denied entry to the United Kingdom starting 25 February 2026, as the government begins enforcing its Electronic Travel Authorisat...

item.title: Iranian Prayer App Hack Sends Surrender Messages During Israeli-US Strikes
item.description: In the wake of joint Israeli and US airstrikes on Tehran, residents across Iran were shocked to receive mysterious push notifications from a hacked prayer-timing app, BadeSaba Calendar. These messages...

item.title: Investigation into Alice Webb’s Death Following Non-Surgical BBL Closes After Suspect Dies
item.description: An investigation into the death of 33-year-old Alice Webb, a mother of five from Gloucestershire, has been formally closed after the death of the main suspect. Webb died on 24 September 2024 after bec...

item.title: Trump Faces Declining Approval and Policy Challenges Ahead of Key State of the Union Address
item.description: President Donald Trump is set to deliver a pivotal State of the Union address at a critical juncture in his second term. Over the past year, he has aggressively pursued a sweeping agenda, implementing...

item.title: Authorities Investigate Canine Distemper Virus Behind Tiger Deaths in Thailand
item.description: In February 2026, 72 tigers died at two tourist parks, Mae Taeng and Mae Rim, in Chiang Mai province, Thailand. Officials have confirmed that the deaths were caused by a virus not known to infect huma...

item.title: Algerians face financial struggles during Ramadan due to rising food prices
item.description: As the holy month of Ramadan begins in Algeria, many people are concerned about how to afford the celebratory feasts that come with it. Despite Algeria's wealth from oil and gas resources, food prices...

item.title: UK Student Loan Repayment Threshold to Freeze Despite Modest Average Increase
item.description: Education Secretary Bridget Phillipson has defended the UK government's plan to freeze the repayment threshold for Plan 2 student loans in England, stating that on average graduates will pay just £8 m...

item.title: Lammy announces expanded AI use in courts alongside plans to limit some jury trials
item.description: UK Justice Secretary and Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy has announced plans to expand the use of artificial intelligence across courts in England and Wales, while also moving forward with proposals...

item.title: Christopher Trybus Denies Manslaughter Charges Over Wife’s Death Amid Allegations of Coercive Control
item.description: A court has heard that Christopher Trybus, 43, from Swindon, Wiltshire, is accused of causing the death of his wife, Tarryn Baird, through sustained coercive and controlling behaviour. Prosecutors all...

item.title: Mayweather and Pacquiao Rematch Highlights Boxing Entertainment Over Competition
item.description: Floyd Mayweather, 49, and Manny Pacquiao, 47, are set to face each other in a professional boxing rematch in Las Vegas on September 19. The fight is widely described as more of a spectacle than a comp...

item.title: Josh Simons Resigns Amid Investigation Into Smear Campaign Allegations
item.description: Josh Simons, a former Cabinet Office minister and Labour MP for Makerfield, resigned following an investigation into his actions at Labour Together, a thinktank he ran. The controversy stemmed from cl...

item.title: Temperatures in the UK expected to reach 18°C on Wednesday amid mild Atlantic winds
item.description: The UK is experiencing an early taste of spring as temperatures rise due to mild south-westerly winds from the Atlantic. Since last weekend, each home nation has recorded its highest temperature of th...

item.title: Ukraine Commemorates War Dead as Conflict Continues into Fifth Year
item.description: Four years after Russia launched its full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the conflict shows no signs of ending. Ukraine continues to defend its territory, particularly in the eastern Donbas region, despit...

item.title: Starmer adopts cautious stance on Iran amidst Trump’s strikes
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer has adopted a cautious stance on the recent US and Israeli attacks on Iran, carefully navigating pressure from both the left and right of British politics. The Labour leader is hesita...

item.title: Epstein's Influence on US Virgin Islands CBP Agents Led to DOJ Investigation
item.description: A year-long investigation into the ties between Jeffrey Epstein and Customs and Border Protection (CBP) agents stationed in the US Virgin Islands revealed a troubling pattern of relationships that per...
